items.prompt: In an 8-bit unsigned integer, what place value does bit position 5 represent when bit positions are numbered from 0 at the least significant bit?

feedback.reason: Bit position k contributes 2^k when it is set.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.value_5: The bit index is an exponent, not the represented value.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.value_16: 16 is the place value of bit position 4.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.value_64: 64 is the place value of bit position 6.

items.prompt: What unsigned decimal value is represented by the 8-bit pattern 10110010?

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.value_77: 77 is not the sum of the set-bit place values.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.value_82: This omits the highest set bit's contribution.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.value_210: This corresponds to a different set of bit positions.

items.prompt: Under 8-bit two's-complement interpretation, what signed value is represented by 10110010?

feedback.reason: The same bit pattern can have different signed and unsigned interpretations.
feedback.details: For an 8-bit two's-complement value with the sign bit set, subtract 2^8 from the unsigned value: 178 - 256 = -78.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.value_178: 178 is the unsigned interpretation of the same bits.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.value_82: The leading one makes the fixed-width signed value negative.

constraints: Use exactly 8 bits and two's-complement signed interpretation.

items.prompt: Which bit is the sign bit in an 8-bit two's-complement integer?

feedback.reason: The most significant bit is the sign bit in fixed-width two's complement.
feedback.details: An 8-bit word has positions 0 through 7, so position 7 determines whether the signed interpretation is negative.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.bit_0: Bit 0 is the least significant value bit.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.bit_6: Bit 6 contributes 64 but is not the sign position.

items.prompt: Which bit positions are selected by the 8-bit mask 00101100?

feedback.reason: A mask selects the positions containing ones.
feedback.details: Reading from the least significant bit as position 0, the set positions are 2, 3, and 5.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.bits_1_2_4: Those positions correspond to a different pattern.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.bits_0_2_3: The least significant bit is zero in this mask.

items.prompt: Using an 8-bit width-limited complement, what is the result of complementing 00001111?

options.text: An unlimited sequence of leading ones followed by 11110000.

feedback.reason: Width-limited complement flips each of the eight declared bits.
feedback.details: The result depends on the width model; without a width, leading-bit behavior is ambiguous.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.infinite_ones: The question explicitly limits the result to 8 bits.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.bits_11111111: Only the four low zeros become ones; the four low ones become zeros.

constraints: Use exactly 8 bits. Complement flips only those 8 positions.

items.prompt: The 8-bit pattern 11111111 is interpreted first as unsigned and then as signed two's complement. Which pair is correct?

feedback.reason: Bits do not carry signedness by themselves; the type interpretation supplies it.
feedback.details: All ones is the maximum unsigned value and -1 in two's complement.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.255_and_255: Signed two's complement interprets the leading-one pattern as negative.

items.prompt: A 4-bit unsigned value 1101 is widened to 8 bits without changing its numeric value. Which representation is correct?

feedback.details: New high-order positions are filled with zeros so the represented value remains 13.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.sign_extended: Sign extension is not used for an unsigned value.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.reversed: Widening does not reverse bit order.

items.prompt: A 4-bit two's-complement value 1101 is sign-extended to 8 bits. Which representation preserves its signed value?

feedback.reason: Signed widening repeats the source sign bit in the new high positions.
feedback.details: The 4-bit pattern 1101 represents -3, and 11111101 is also -3 in 8-bit two's complement.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.zero_extended: Zero extension changes the signed value from -3 to 13.

items.prompt: Which expression denotes a one-hot mask selecting zero-based bit position k when the value 1 can be shifted safely within the declared width?

options.text: A word with every bit set.

feedback.reason: A one-hot mask contains exactly one set bit at the selected position.
feedback.details: Moving the low one bit left by k positions produces place value 2^k.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.k_shift_one: This doubles k rather than selecting position k.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.one_plus_k: Addition does not generally create a one-hot pattern.

items.prompt: A 9-bit pattern 100000001 is truncated to its low 8 bits. What 8-bit pattern remains?

feedback.reason: Fixed-width truncation discards positions at or above the target width.
feedback.details: The low eight positions of 100000001 contain only bit 0 set.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.bits_10000000: The high ninth bit is discarded rather than moved.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.bits_10000001: That would keep nine-bit information in an eight-bit word.

constraints: Retain exactly the least significant 8 bits.

items.prompt: Which statements about fixed-width two's-complement representation are correct? Select all that apply.

options.text: The same bits can have different signed and unsigned numeric interpretations.
A word of all ones represents -1 when interpreted as signed two's complement.
The most significant bit is the sign bit.
The numeric signed value is independent of the declared width.

feedback.reason: Two's complement is a fixed-width interpretation model.
feedback.details: Width determines the sign position and the negative weight of that bit.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.width_irrelevant: The same low bits can represent different values after widening or truncation.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.same_bits_diff_meaning: Signedness changes interpretation, not stored bits.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.all_ones_minus_one: This holds at every positive fixed width.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.msb_sign: It carries negative weight in signed interpretation.

items.prompt: Which statements correctly describe a bit mask? Select all that apply.

options.text: Set mask bits identify selected positions.
A mask may contain one or multiple set bits.
The semantic meaning of a selected position comes from the surrounding contract.
Every mask is inherently a signed negative number.

feedback.reason: A mask is a positional selection pattern whose meaning is contract-defined.
feedback.details: Its bit pattern can be used under signed or unsigned storage without changing which positions are selected.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.automatically_signed: A mask is a bit pattern; numeric signedness depends on interpretation.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.positions_selected: A one marks a position of interest.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.can_select_many: Masks are not limited to one-hot patterns.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.meaning_contextual: A bit may represent a feature, permission, resource, or numeric place.

items.prompt: Which operations or interpretations require an explicit width model to avoid ambiguity? Select all that apply.

options.text: Interpreting a negative two's-complement bit pattern.
AND of two explicitly written finite nonnegative low-bit patterns.

feedback.reason: Width-sensitive operations depend on where the word begins and ends.
feedback.details: Simple AND of explicit nonnegative finite patterns can be reasoned about directly over the shown bits.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.low_nonnegative_and: The displayed finite positions are sufficient when no complement or sign interpretation is involved.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.complement: The number of high bits being flipped matters.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.negative_bits: The sign position depends on width.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.truncation: The target width defines what remains.

items.prompt: Which contributions are present in the unsigned pattern 01010110? Select all that apply.

feedback.reason: Unsigned value is the sum of place values for all set positions.
feedback.details: The remaining set bit contributes 2, giving a total of 86.

items.prompt: Arrange the steps for interpreting a fixed-width unsigned bit pattern.

elements.text: Number positions from 0 at the least significant bit.
Map each set position k to 2^k.

feedback.reason: Unsigned interpretation is a positional sum.
feedback.details: Starting from the least significant position prevents reversed place-value mistakes.

items.prompt: Arrange the steps for interpreting an 8-bit two's-complement pattern whose most significant bit is one.

elements.text: Compute the unsigned value of the eight bits.
Observe that bit 7 is set, so signed interpretation is negative.

feedback.reason: For a fixed-width two's-complement word, negative signed value equals unsigned value minus 2^width.
feedback.details: This method makes the width dependency explicit.

items.prompt: An algorithm inspects every bit of an arbitrary-precision nonnegative integer represented with w significant bits and uses only a counter. Select time and auxiliary-space complexity.
constraints: The representation width w may grow with the input.

feedback.reason: Bit operations are not automatically O(1) when integer width is part of the input.
feedback.details: Scanning all w represented positions costs O(w), while a fixed number of counters uses constant auxiliary space.
